observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
(a) The Municipal Accountant and GSO-designate to reconcile their records on
PPE and address the unreconciled difference of ₱174,485,042.15. The
Municipal Accountant ensure that PPE items recorded in the books are
supported with PPELCs and property cards on the part of the GSO.
(b) Municipal Accountant to:
1) Examine the RPCPPE submitted particularly those PPEs reported in the
physical count and ensure that those are supported with property cards
on the part of the GSO-Office and recorded in the books in the part of
the Accounting.
(c) GSO-designate/Municipal Treasurer, and in coordination with the
Inventory Committee to:
1) Conduct an annual physical inventory of all PPE items, including local
road networks and infrastructure assets;
2) Regularly update its records and prepare RPCPPE and RPCLRN
following the format prescribed in Annex 49 of NGAS for LGUs, and
Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008, respectively; and
3) Prepare and maintain PCs for each class of PPE to record the
acquisition, description, custody, disposal, and other information about
the asset.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Assess the composition and capacities of the members of the Inventory
Committee, and take the necessary course of action, such as delineating the
duties and functions of the members of the Inventory Committee, or appointing
one personnel from each department/office as a member of the Committee who
would lead the conduct of the physical count and preparation of the RPCI per
department/office;
b) Strictly require the department heads to submit the RPCI per type of inventory
in their custody/office to the Inventory Committee for consolidation and
validation within the period required by Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for
LGUs;
c) Require the Inventory Committee/GSO to:
1) Observe the procedures laid down in the Manual for the receipt, issuance
and utilization of inventories; specifically, (i) stop the practice of using the
PR as basis for the direct issuance to or receipt of delivered supplies to the
requesting department/office, (ii) require the requesting department/office
to accomplish the RIS, (iii) consolidate the RIS, and prepare and submit the
SSMI to the MAO for recording or updating of the Inventory balances in
the books; and (iv) direct the Inventory Committee/GSO to maintain the
property cards and stock cards, and periodically reconcile those with the
ledger cards maintained by the Accounting Department;
2) Conduct the semestral physical count of all inventories under their custody,
and those that are under the custody of the different departments/offices,
and prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team not later than July 31
and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively;
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the supplies ledger card per
commodity/stock as required by the guidelines, and periodically reconcile it
with the stock cards and property cards maintained by the GSO for checks and
balances; and
e) Require the GSO and MAO to reconcile the balances found in the financial
statements against the results of the physical count to prevent misstatement of
inventory balance.

We recommended that Management:
a) Send demand letters to the concerned AOs to enforce the liquidation of their
cash advance;
b) Require the Accountable Officers concerned to settle/liquidate immediately
their outstanding cash advances and the concerned officials to strictly
adhere to the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization, and
liquidation of cash advances provided under COA Circular 97-002;
c) Enforce the appropriate sanctions provided by rules and regulations upon
failure by the concerned AOs to liquidate their cash advances despite receipt
of the demand letter issued by the Municipal Accountant;
d) Ensure that outstanding cash advances are liquidated prior to separation
from office by any appointed or elected officer or employee; otherwise, the
personnel who signed the clearance will be held accountable for the liability
of the official or employee who separated from the service; and
e) Office of the Municipal Accountant to reconcile and establish the correct
cash advance balance of the retired employee, particularly the previous
MSWD Head, and ensure that schedules of cash advance, notes to FS, and
subsidiary ledgers all show the same amounts.

(1) Require the head of various offices to oversee the conduct of physical count of existing PPEs found in their respective offices and prepare the report using Annex A of the cited Circular for verification of the Inventory Committee; (2) Direct the Inventory Committee to update the RPCPPE using the data provided by various offices to reflect the actual count and conditions of the PPE items maintained by the Municipality; and (3) Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPELCs for all items of PPE and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger.
observations.reason: Not Reiterated in Part II of this report. Implemented Not Reiterated in Part II of this report. Implemented

observations.recommendation: (4) Convene with the representative of the WSP and communicate the lapses as to compliance with the provisions set out in Section 8.3 and Section 7 of the MOA; (5) Agree in writing whether the responsibility of collecting the outstanding MILAWUD consumptions shall be continued by the WSP or reverted to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer; (6) Determine and enforce other control measures to ensure the collection of the remaining arrears from concerned MILAWUD consumers; (7) Require the WSP to submit the summary report of water consumption starting from its date of operation from July 2021 until December 2021 and January to December 2022 so that the Municipal Accountant will be able to bill the unpaid franchise fee, and thereafter a monthly report of the water consumption in CY 2023 to allow the Office of the Municipal Accountant to include in the billing statement the franchise fee due for the period; (8) Direct the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the payment received from the WSP and submit the related reports to the Office of the Municipal Accountant to properly account for the payment and reflect any adjustments in the billing statement; and (9) Prospectively, timely coordinate any concern to the WSP to properly address any issues concerning the agreements and conditions laid down in the MOA.
observations.reason: Not Reiterated in the part II of the report Implemented Not Reiterated in the part II of the report Implemented Not Reiterated in the part II of the report Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the: a) Municipal Treasurer to: (10) Coordinate and reconcile their records with the Office of the Municipal Assessor concerning reports affecting the ownership, valuation, and recognition of real properties within the Municipality’s jurisdiction; (11) Review the Assessment Roll and the Journal of Approved Transactions submitted by the Office of the Municipal Assessor monthly and regularly update the RPTAR/RPTIC; (12) Prepare the summary list of RPT and SET receivables based on the updated RPTAR/RPTIC and submit it together with the list of taxpayers and their corresponding taxes to the Office of the Municipal Accountant at year-end or at the beginning of the succeeding year to be the basis for setting the receivable; and (13) Regularly update the RPTAR/RPTIC and reconcile with the Office of the Municipal Accountant to determine the correct value of the RPT and SET receivables. b) Municipal Assessor to: (14) Regularly update and furnish the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with reports related to transactions affecting the assessment and recognition of real properties within the Municipality’s jurisdiction; and (15) Reconcile their records at least annually to ensure that records are properly updated and to facilitate the establishment of correct RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year. (16) Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with respect to the unpaid RPT and SET to properly establish and substantiate the reported receivables in the book.
observations.reason: Not Reconciliation of RPT/SET not yet effected between Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Accountant’s Officce.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (17) Direct the Inventory Committee to determine the status of the unregistered lands and coordinate with the concerned departments in complying with the requirements for its registration; and (18) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the entry made to record the dumping of structural fill from the Land account to the Slaughterhouse to properly account for the correct value of land and the related assets.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022
observations.reason: But as to the exact status, the Audit Team still awaits the reply by the Municipal Assessor upon audit inquiry.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management and all others concerned in the procurement, inspection of deliveries, processing, approval, and payment of claims: (19) Provide adequate and proper documentation for procurements of infrastructures and goods; (20) Withhold the 10 percent retention money in accordance with Sections 5.3.c and 6 of Annex “E” of the RIRR of RA No. 9184; (21) Scrutinize the bidding documents submitted by the bidders, and ensure compliance with the eligibility requirements provided in the RIRR of RA No. 9184, which includes, among others, an NFCC at least equal to the ABC; and (22) Review the existing roles and responsibilities delegated and/or assumed by the different LGU offices and its key officers, and delineate incompatible or complementing functions, as appropriate.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (23) Strictly require the full and prompt settlement of the cash advances as provided in the pertinent COA Circulars, otherwise, enforce the appropriate sanctions; (24) Send final demand letters to persons with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their accounts; (25) Stop or refuse additional cash advances to officers and/or employees unless previous ones have been settled; and (26) Request write-off of unliquidated balances of deceased accountable officers in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.reason: During the exit conference, the Management averred that demand letters were sent but the final demand letters were still to be sent to the Accountable officers. No request were received by the Audit Team for the write off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (27) Through the Office of the Municipal Accountant, confirm with the DOF-MDFO and the PVB the balance of existing loans and the related charges to the Municipality to ensure that the recorded balances in the books are accurate, and the charges imposed upon the LGU are valid; With the aid of the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator: (28) Prospectively, conduct a thorough study of the proposed developmental and socio-economic projects, and present the same for the concurrence of the local government development council; (29) Together with the Implementing Office, monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development programs, projects, and activities in the Municipality by preparing a report showing the actual accomplishment or status of the projects vis-à-vis the target, to ensure effective and efficient use of the funds; and (30) Develop plans to address the problems which prevent the continued operation of the existing projects or determine alternative use thereof to maximize its purpose.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022
observations.reason: Confirmation of balances with the DOF and PVB was not conducted. Current balance of loans was shown in the Financial statements of the LGU. Proper monitoring of infrastructure projects particularly the LDF projects was not prepared in the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management review the legal bases, evaluate the necessity of maintaining the subject accounts, and cause their immediate updating, or closure, as appropriate.
observations.managementAction: Not The Municipal Treasurer was able to update as of Thai writing two of the dormant accounts into current accounts.
observations.reason: The other dormant accounts will still be reviewed if possible to subsequently close it if no longer needed by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (32) Ensure that compliance on various government programs implemented by RAs be promptly made so that the LGU may not miss any financial assistance that is being given to the implementing LGU thus, LGU fund could be saved and used for other priority projects; and (33) Explain in writing the charges in the CBMS which do not align with the LDF guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (34) Direct the MEO to explain the determination of the contract duration specified in the Program of Works / Contract Agreement for all the LDF infrastructure projects undertaken for CY 2022; (35) Prepare the PERT/CPM network diagram before the preparation of the Bidding Documents to ensure that the contract time computation is based on the PERT/CPM Network Diagram, particularly prepared by the MEO; (36) Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that the contract time computation is supported with a PERT/CPM Network Diagram; (37) Require MEO officers/personnel concerned to provide a program of works and needs analysis for each of the infrastructure projects included in the Local Development Plan (LDP) so that a more realistic estimate of costs is presented as the basis for the approved appropriations/budget; (38) Ensure that no program of work for any project shall be approved without adequate detailed engineering; (39) Impose equivalent liquidated damages in case of Contractor’s delay/default and suspension of work of construction projects shall be granted based only on grounds authorized under the RIRR of RA 9184, or otherwise, the transaction may be suspended in audit; and (40) Direct the MLDC to ensure that projects being considered in the LDP were supported with complete detailed engineering particularly the Program of Work with PERT/CPM Network Diagram before its inclusion in the LDP.
observations.managementAction: The MEO explained that PERT CPM were Not prepared but was not attached to the DVs.
observations.reason: An audit observation was issued pertaining to the attachment of complete supporting documents particularly the LDF in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (41) Direct the BAC to ensure the widest dissemination of procurement information so that the contract will not be limited to the same contractor/supplier only; and (42) Direct the BAC and BAC TWG to conduct the post-qualification procedure carefully and thoroughly in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the IRR, otherwise, disqualify the contractor that is not compliant with the requirements.

We recommended that the Management: (43) Direct the Local Development Council to provide a document/justification in writing on the shift of purpose of the project from the public market into an evacuation center and multi-purpose building; (44) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide legal basis or authorization on the disbursement and charging of the additional expenses for the construction of the evacuation center formerly constructed as a public market in the project improvement of the multi-purpose building; (45) Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and Municipal Local Development Council (MLDC) and Municipal Engineering Office to explain in writing the implementation of two projects (construction and improvement) at the same time while referring to the same project only; and (46) Direct the MPDO to ensure that projects identified to be included in the LDP were sufficiently described in complete and specific details like construction of the second floor, improvement of the first floor or evacuation center or multi-purpose building or slaughterhouse, etc., to avoid confusion.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022
observations.reason: Justification in writing was provided by the LDC and yet to be evaluated by the Audit Team. The Municipal Budget Officer awaits the legal basis to be provided by the Budget Officer. Proper and specific project description of LDF projects were still being practiced by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to: (47) Review the revenue collection targets issued by the BLGF, and implement strategies to achieve the target; otherwise, request for target adjustments, if necessary; (48) Develop and implement measures to effectively improve the collection policies being enforced in the Municipality; and (49) Issue demand letters to taxpayers and other concerned individuals with delinquent taxes or unpaid charges.
observations.reason: The revenue collection efficiency as of December 31,2023 showed a 89% collection percentage. Letters were not issued for delinquent taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (50) Include the unexpended DRRMF for the past five years in its annual LDRRMFIP to fund the continuation of partially implemented and unimplemented DRRM PPAs, and to be charged from the other identified PPAs by the LGU under the four thematic areas of DRRM; (51) Observe adequate planning of DRRM PPAs, including but not limited to the specific identification of the type, volume, and estimated cost of items to be purchased or works to be accomplished under each PPA, inclusion or consolidation thereof to the APP, and determination of the timing and proper mode of procurement, to facilitate the prompt and economic implementation of such PPAs; and (52) Comply with the pertinent provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 on selecting the proper mode of procurement and attach complete supporting documents for such selected mode of procurement, as provided in Section 9 of the COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: An audit observation regarding the PPAs to be charged from the unexpended balances of LDRRMF under Trust Liabilities amounting to ₱4,963,814.04 were not identified and included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) was issued in the audit of CY 2023 accounts.
observations.reason: Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) to be charged from the unexpended balances of LDRRMF under Trust Liabilities amounting to ₱4,963,814.04 were not identified and included in the LDRRMFIP was issued in the audit of CY 2023 accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (53) Stop the discretionary hiring of consultants, and observe the proper procurement process in the hiring of the same as provided in the pertinent provisions of the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Consulting Services, and the RIRR of RA No. 9184; (54) Carefully assess the need of hiring a consultant to avoid duplication of functions, and prepare the requisite Terms of Reference; (55) Prepare and submit all relevant supporting documents from procurement until payment of consultants; and (56) Furnish the Audit Team with the written authority of the Solicitor General and written concurrence of the COA on the hiring of a legal consultant.
observations.reason: No noted submission of the relevant supporting documents was observed. The written authority of the Solicitor General and written concurrence of the COA on the hiring of a legal consultant were not furnished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (57) Issue an Acknowledgement Receipt for in-kind donations for DRRM signed by the Head of the Agency or his/ her authorized representative; (58) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Registry of Donated Relief Goods – DRRM, to reflect the full cost of the ending inventory of goods/vaccines received and issued, and record in the books the cost of donated PPE less its total accumulated depreciation if there is any; (59) Instruct the Property and Supply Unit to inspect the donations to the Municipality and maintain the Property Cards of the same for check and balance with the accounting Unit; (60) Instruct the RHU to prepare and submit a monthly report to the Accounting Office on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from outside sources; and (61) Instruct the DRRM to prepare the reports/ records expected of them in terms of in-kind donations as prescribed in the Circular.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer prepared Acknowledgement Receipts; Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE ledger cards and registry; property inspection and recording by RHU and DRRM to be complied. Some actions remain to be fully implemented as noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (62) Convene with the representatives of the WSP and clarify the intention of the parties concerning the conditions outlined in Section 14.1 (Conditions Precedent) and Section 2.m.l (Schedule of Works of the WSP); (63) Put in writing any clarifications and require the settlement of the related loans according to the agreement of both parties; and (64) Designate a Focal person who shall coordinate with the representatives of the WSP to easily communicate concerns and resolve issues affecting the conditions of the agreement.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in the part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (65) Direct the Implementing Offices to submit the report on the distribution of welfare goods and agricultural supplies duly acknowledged by recipients and prospectively, require the concerned Offices to timely submit these reports to the Audit Team for validation. (66) Require the Inventory Committee, together with various heads of Offices, to establish guidelines and/or policies to ensure that the inventories are properly monitored and accounted for while the LGU still works on addressing the issues which prevented the implementation of the inventory process; and Direct the Accounting Office to: (67) Verify from the concerned Offices, with the aid of the Inventory Committee, the status of these inventories to properly reflect the correct valuation and condition of the inventories in the books; and (68) Ensure that all transactions related to inventories are properly supported with complete documentation before drawing journal entries to record and/or make adjustments thereto.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022
observations.reason: The said balance amounting to ₱2,574,817.55 were still included in the Inventory balance as of December 31,2023. The Municipal Accounting Office is awaiting the distribution list, RIS and SSMI to support the expense adjustment on Inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the GFPS to: (69) Use the prescribed GPB format, and communicate in writing any changes therein to the DILG; (70) Conduct the appropriate HGDG tests on the planned and implemented major programs of the LGU to determine its relevance, impact, and attributable costs to GAD; (71) Prepare a GAD Accomplishment Report based on the GAD Plan and Budget previously reviewed and endorsed by DILG, fill out columns therein with appropriate and adequate information, and attach relevant supporting documents; and (72) Implement a defined monitoring and evaluation system to properly assess the status and impact of GAD PPAs on gender mainstreaming.
observations.reason: The HGDG tests were not conducted by the GADFS; an audit observation on the GADFS and effectivity of GAD PPAs was issued in the audit of CY 2023 accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (73) Remind the LSB to review the budget and its components to ensure that all disbursements are allowable charges against the SEF and that the fund will be utilized for priority programs and projects; (74) Direct the LSB and/or appropriate Office to submit the Disbursement Voucher and the related supporting documents of the two transactions identified above (Check Nos. 2522181 and 2522183) to support the validity of the claim, and to prevent the issuance of Notice of Suspension; (75) Direct the LSB, with the aid of the Office of the Municipal Engineer, to submit an inspection report, together with the supporting documents related to the implementation of the construction and/or repair projects; and (76) Submit the necessary supporting documents, such as pictures of procured items, distribution list to support the validity of disbursement and ensure that all other transactions are appended with what is the minimum required supporting documents.
observations.reason: No noted submission of the mentioned DVs; retrieval of the DVs and submission and subsequent evaluation and proper audit action will be done by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (76) Refrain from resorting to Alternative Method of procurement for catering services or other procurement wherein no emergency is sought to be addressed and strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 prescribing the guidelines and regarding resort to said alternative mode of procurement; and (77) Submit the necessary supporting documents, such as pictures of procured items, distribution list to support the validity of disbursement and ensure that all other transactions are appended with what is the minimum required supporting documents.
observations.reason: Frequent recourse to alternative methods of procurement, particularly SVP, was still observed in CY 2022 procurements. Documents to support procurement and utilization/distribution of supplies and other inventories were incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to: (78) Individually evaluate the balances reflected in the books, and, if found to be with remaining balances, revisit the MOA or coordinate with the Source Agency to determine whether the balances have to be returned to the Source Agency, and effect the return of the same, as appropriate.

We recommended that Management direct the: (79) Municipal Development Council to ensure that PPAs in the Local Development Plan are supported with the necessary in-depth study and planning documents before its plan inclusion; and (80) Refrain from identifying and including PPAs in the Local Development Plan in general categories, rather should be specifically identified.
observations.reason: Not all PPAs are supported with in-depth study before plan inclusion. The MPDC committed to have necessary in-depth study and funds will be allocated for the necessary in-depth study of the PPAs before their inclusion in the plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the: (81) Municipal Development Council to carefully plan and specifically identify the PPAs in the Local Development Plan and ensure its inclusion in the budget and Annual and/or Supplemental Investment Program, to facilitate the monitoring and timely implementation of the priority projects; and (82) Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that an Ordinance was issued by the Sanggunian approving the reprogramming of PPAs to more specific items, before obligating the fund for those projects described in generic terms or with lump sum appropriations.
observations.reason: The generic description of PPAs has been the practice of the LGU; the Municipal Development Council commits to carefully design and precisely define the PPAs in the Local Development Plan, as well as assure their inclusion in the budget and Annual and/or Supplementary Investment Program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (83) Prepare, review and update annually its LDRRMFIP, and include in the Plan, under a separate caption, the PPAs to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years stored in the special trust fund.
observations.reason: An audit observation regarding the Programs, Projects, Activities (PPAs) to be charged from the unexpended balances of LDRRMF under Trust Liabilities was issued in this year’s audit of CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: (84) Prepare a clear, comprehensive and well-defined implementing guidelines on the LGU AICs program and all other assistance and institutionalize the same to formalize its adoption to ensure that only qualified and eligible indigent beneficiaries were given the type, kind and amount of assistance they needed after the assessment, validation and recommendation of the MSWDO; (85) Initiate a comprehensive review of the LGU’s Program on AICS and all other assistance to the poor, vulnerable and marginalized individuals and families; (86) Thru the MSWDO, to conduct a prior study, screening, verification, assessment and recommendation of qualified recipients of the program and install and implement a control mechanism that would monitor on duplication of beneficiaries and the assistance, qualification and eligibility of beneficiaries and funds available for disbursement; and (87) Thru the MSWDO, to install a database encoding system like a Crisis Intervention Monitoring system as stated in the DSWD guidelines instead of a logbook that can easily summarize data in a particular period as well as to fast checking of names of beneficiaries to avoid duplication and minimize manual errors.
observations.reason: The Office assesses and gives necessary assistance to eligible individuals and families. The Office conducts prior study, screening, assessment and recommendation of qualified recipients of the program. The Office maintains logbook and other data; implementation of an electronic database is pending additional resources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (88) Prospectively, require as part of the documentary requirements for the payment of 15% mobilization cost, the submission of an irrevocable standby letter of credit, a bank guarantee, or a surety bond to safeguard the Municipality for possible risk of non-recovery of advances.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to: (89) Keep a detailed Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations showing the relevant amounts per function, program, project and activity; and (90) Prepare and maintain the Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for each of the four classes of expenditures, and submit electronic copies thereof to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The details necessary to support the balances in the RAAO were not furnished to the Audit Team. What was submitted has a lumpsum RAAO showing the accounts with assigned amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (91) Convene with the LSB and agree on the timeline of the preparation and approval of the SEF budget following the indicative period of relevant activities in the cited Joint Circular; (92) Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to work together in the preparation of the certified statement of SEF income to properly establish a more realistic basis of the available fund for appropriation; (93) Require the LSB to submit a project proposal for the acquisition of the 75 inches Smart Flat Screen TV to establish the purpose of the purchase and to ensure its utilization for priority programs and projects of the Municipal School Board; (94) Direct the LSB to explain regarding the purchase of items totaling ₱68,648.00 which did not fall under the priority spending chargeable against the SEF; (95) Require the LSB to present the proposed expenditures for the budget year in detail, by PAP, by allotment class and per school; and (96) Remind the LSB to review the budget and its components to ensure that the fund will be utilized for priority programs and projects and will be prepared in accordance with the requirements of the relevant laws, and regulations.
observations.reason: No project proposal was submitted for the 75 inches Smart Flat Screen TV; some expenditures not falling under priority spending were not properly justified; the LSB was reminded to prepare and submit required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (97) Direct the Environmental Management Specialist to review and update annually the provisions of the 10-Year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan, and ensure compliance to the requirements of the DENR-EMB V to facilitate its prompt approval and implementation; and (98) Consider the inclusion of Representatives from the various industries or group cited in the Act to further improve its plans through the integration of policies and programs which may developed through consultations with these sectors.
observations.managementAction: An audit observation was issued in this year’s audit CY 2023 about the SWM in which the SWM plan for CY 2022-2031 remained unapproved and were of utmost priority in revision.
observations.reason: Consultations with the barangays and other stakeholders were done in the formulation of the 10-Year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (99) require the Municipal Treasurer, as the head of the Treasury Office, to exercise proper management, particularly to impose strong internal control on collections and deposits, of the funds of the LGU; and (100) advise the Municipal Treasurer, as well as the Collectors/Tellers and Liquidating Officer, to look into the 183 identified transactions to specifically identify the root cause of the unusual balance and rectify and avoid the same in the future.
observations.reason: Identification of some transactions is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to: (101) produce the missing AFs or determine the status of the unaccounted forms and provide evidence to support its issuance, cancellation or existence; (102) monitor the inventory of accountable forms and make a periodic reconciliation of the actual inventory and the records; (103) prepare, maintain and periodically update record books, Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and other relevant reports; (104) strictly adhere to the provisions of the above cited Manual and other relevant rules and regulations; and (105) initiate administrative sanctions to those employees responsible for the unaccounted AFs.
observations.reason: The status or condition of the unaccounted forms were identified/monitored by the Municipal Treasurer. RAAF were prepared, maintained and updated. Some required consolidated monthly RAAF were not strictly complied with.

observations.recommendation: (106) We recommended that the LGU strictly adhere to the guidelines and utilization of BGCM provided under DBM LBC No. 25 or for any grants/funds transferred to the LGU for specific purposes.
observations.managementAction: Noted that the grant was intended for food and their (Barangay Health Emergency Response Team) food allowances.
observations.reason: The intention of giving them assistance is for food and other LGU personnel, including the BHERT, since they are also frontliners of the municipality. However, the grant was given in the form of a check (considered as cash). The Audit Team acknowledged the intention, but the LGU must strictly follow the guidelines set forth by LBC No. 25 in the utilization of the BGCM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Mayor, ensure that before granting Hazard Pay to the personnel of MSWDO, Accomplishment Reports on Occupational Risk Activities, or any equivalent document, adequately contains the information necessary to support the personnel’s actual exposure to hazards and hardships.
observations.reason: DTR and Accomplishment Reports signed by the Municipal Mayor were already submitted to the Accounting Office, the hazard pay and subsistence of the registered social workers are being processed.

observations.recommendation: (108) We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the above-mentioned disbursement vouchers and cancelled checks for the latter to take up the transactions in the books of the agency and subsequently submit it to the Audit Team for audit to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.
observations.reason: The subject DVs and mentioned DVs were partially submitted; accountability for these unsubmitted DV’s was made by management and verification of the unsubmitted transactions was conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide for the disclosure of the road network of the LGU in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with COA Circular 2015-008.
observations.reason: No Road Network disclosures provided in the FS ending December 31, 2023. Pending details from Municipal Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Point System to: (110) set up and maintain the GAD database with adequate, updated and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; (111) undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects, and activities are responsive to the said issues; and (112) revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the head of various offices to oversee the conduct of physical count of existing PPEs found in their respective offices and prepare the report using Annex A of the cited Circular for verification of the Inventory Committee;
Direct the Inventory Committee to update the RPCPPE using the data provided by various offices to reflect the actual count and conditions of the PPE items maintained by the Municipality; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPELCs for all items of PPE and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger.

We recommended that the Management direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate and reconcile their records with the Office of the Municipal Assessor concerning reports affecting the ownership, valuation, and recognition of real properties within the Municipality’s jurisdiction;
Review the Assessment Roll and the Journal of Approved Transactions submitted by the Office of the Municipal Assessor monthly and regularly update the RPTAR/RPTIC;
Prepare the summary list of RPT and SET receivables based on the updated RPTAR/RPTIC and submit it together with the list of taxpayers and their corresponding taxes to the Office of the Municipal Accountant at year-end or at the beginning of the succeeding year to be the basis for setting the receivable; and
Regularly update the RPTAR/RPTIC and reconcile with the Office of the Municipal Accountant to determine the correct value of the RPT and SET receivables.
Municipal Assessor to:
Regularly update and furnish the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with reports related to transactions affecting the assessment and recognition of real properties within the Municipality’s jurisdiction; and
Reconcile their records at least annually to ensure that records are properly updated and to facilitate the establishment of correct RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year.
Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with respect to the unpaid RPT and SET to properly establish and substantiate the reported receivables in the book.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Inventory Committee to determine the status of the unregistered lands and coordinate with the concerned departments in complying with the requirements for its registration; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the entry made to record the dumping of structural fill from the Land account to the Slaughterhouse to properly account for the correct value of land and the related assets.
We recommended that Management review the legal bases, evaluate the necessity of maintaining the subject accounts, and cause their immediate updating, or closure, as appropriate.

We recommended that the Management:
Ensure that compliance on various government programs implemented by RAs be promptly made so that the LGU may not miss any financial assistance that is being given to the implementing LGU thus, LGU fund could be saved and used for other priority projects; and
Explain in writing the charges in the CBMS which do not align with the LDF guidelines.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the BAC to ensure the widest dissemination of procurement information so that the contract will not be limited to the same contractor/supplier only; and
Direct the BAC and BAC TWG to conduct the post-qualification procedure carefully and thoroughly in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the IRR, otherwise, disqualify the contractor that is not compliant with the requirements.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Review the revenue collection targets issued by the BLGF, and implement strategies to achieve the target; otherwise, request for target adjustments, if necessary;
Develop and implement measures to effectively improve the collection policies being enforced in the Municipality; and
Issue demand letters to taxpayers and other concerned individuals with delinquent taxes or unpaid charges.

We recommended that the Management:
Include the unexpended DRRMF for the past five years in its annual LDRRMFIP to fund the continuation of partially implemented and unimplemented DRRM PPAs, and other identified PPAs by the LGU under the four thematic areas of DRRM;
Observe adequate planning of DRRM PPAs, including but not limited to the specific identification of the type, volume, and estimated cost of items to be purchased or works to be accomplished under each PPA, inclusion or consolidation thereof to the APP, and determination of the timing and proper mode of procurement, to facilitate the prompt and economic implementation of such PPAs; and
Comply with the pertinent provisions of the RIRR of RA. No. 9184 on selecting the proper mode of procurement and attach complete supporting documents for such selected mode of procurement, as provided in Section 9 of the COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Management:
Convene with the representatives of the WSP and clarify the intention of the parties concerning the conditions outlined in Section 14.1 (Conditions Precedent) and Section 2.m.l (Schedule of Works of the WSP);
Put in writing any clarifications and require the settlement of the related loans according to the agreement of both parties; and
Designate a Focal person who shall coordinate with the representatives of the WSP to easily communicate concerns and resolve issues affecting the conditions of the agreement.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Implementing Offices to submit the report on the distribution of welfare goods and agricultural supplies duly acknowledged by recipients and prospectively, require the concerned Offices to timely submit these reports to substantiate the distribution, transfer, consumption, or disposal of inventories;
Require the Inventory Committee, together with various heads of Offices, to establish guidelines and/or policies to ensure that the inventories are properly monitored and accounted for while the LGU still works on addressing the issues which prevented the implementation of the inventory process; and
Direct the Accounting Office to:
Verify from the concerned Offices, with the aid of the Inventory Committee, the status of these inventories to properly reflect the correct valuation and condition of the inventories in the books; and
Ensure that all transactions related to inventories are properly supported with complete documentation before drawing journal entries to record and/or make adjustments thereto.

We recommended that Management:
Remind the LSB to review the budget and its components to ensure that all disbursements are allowable charges against the SEF and that the fund will be utilized for priority programs and projects;
Direct the LSB and/or appropriate Office to submit the Disbursement Voucher and the related supporting documents of the two transactions identified above (Check Nos. 2522181 and 2522183) to support the validity of the claim, and to prevent the issuance of Notice of Suspension; and
Direct the LSB, with the aid of the Office of the Municipal Engineer, to submit an inspection report, together with the supporting documents related to the implementation of the construction and/or repair projects.

We recommended that the LGU, thru the Accounting Department, to:

Stop the practice of making year-end adjusting entries to record the deposits in transit, and outstanding checks in the books; and

Restate the 2021 balances of cash and accounts payable accounts in its comparative FS for CY 2022, in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that Management:

Submit justification and legal basis for the release of financial assistance to MDRMMO personnel;

Refrain from resorting to Alternative Method of procurement for catering services or other procurement wherein no emergency is sought to be addressed and strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 prescribing the guidelines regarding resort to said alternative mode of procurement; and

Submit the necessary supporting documents such as pictures of procured items, distribution list to support the validity of disbursement and ensure that all other transactions are appended with what is the minimum required supporting documents.

observations.managementAction:
Food and transportation allowance were provided to LGU employees who still reported for work during ECQ.


Management justified that the disbursements granted to certain personnel of the local government was supposedly intended as food and transportation expenses of medical and other LGU personnel directly involved in the implementation of COVID related PPAs and expenses which is enumerated as Item 3.5.6 in DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125. Due to the enforcement of Enhanced Community Quarantine throughout the entire Luzon from March 17 to May 31,2020, the local government of Milaor was in short of personnel as there were no means of public transportation and due to the risk of being exposes to the deadly and contagious COVID 19. This agency has issued Memorandum for those personnel to on skeleton workforce and receive such amount for their transportation expenses and meal allowances as there were also no open eateries during such time.
observations.reason:
Among others, Section 3.5.6 of LBC No. 125 provides that the BGCM shall be exclusively used for the food, transportation, and accommodation expenses of medical personnel and other LGU personnel directly involved in the implementation of COVID 19 related PPAs and expenses. The LGU granted cash ranging from ₱2,000.00 to ₱25,000.00 to 153 LGU officials and employees for their food and transportation allowance. But supporting documents and justification to support the validity of the grant and varying amounts given were inadequate.

The Audit Team maintains its stand on the grant given to LGU personnel and employees. If given as food and transportation allowance, there should have been a fixed amount to all instead of varying amount given. Thus, considering all of the above rejoinder, the audit team is not convinced on the justification given.

Submit justification and legal basis for the release of financial assistance to MDRMMO personnel;

Submit the necessary supporting documents such as pictures of procured items, distribution list to support the validity of disbursement and ensure that all other transactions are appended with what is the minimum required supporting documents.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Individually evaluate the balances reflected in the books, and, if found to be with remaining balances, revisit the MOA or coordinate with the Source Agency to determine whether the balances have to be returned to the Source Agency, and effect the return of the same, as appropriate;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Property Officer to:
conduct a complete annual physical count of all properties of the LGU, and subsequently prepare and submit the report to the Audit Team on or before January 31 of the succeeding year;
prepare and maintain Property Ledger Cards for each PPE account and reconcile the same with accounting records; and
maintain updated files of Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) of all properties issued to determine accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to release and distribute the shares due to the barangay from the proceeds of basic real property tax (RPT) within the period prescribed under Section 271(d) of R.A. No.7160.

We recommended that Management require the concerned personnel/department to:
secure and maintain a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement or the implementing guidelines governing the implementation and liquidation of grants received from the national agencies;
monitor the status of projects funded by national agencies and timely coordinate with the source agency to ensure that any grants be settled accordingly; and
review the related entries and make corresponding adjustments, if necessary, to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements.
We recommended that the LGU strictly adhere to the guidelines and utilization of BGCM provided under DBM LBC No. 25 or for any grants/funds transferred to the LGU for specific purposes.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Mayor, ensure that before granting Hazard Pay to the personnel of MSWDO, Accomplishment Report on Occupational Risk Activities, or any equivalent document, adequately contains the information necessary to support the personnel’s actual exposure to hazards and hardships. This control would guarantee the validity and propriety of the claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate an official to prepare the Approved Budget for the Contract for all projects of the LGU to ensure that the amount of ABC in the Invitation to Bid is duly substantiated.
We further recommended that a copy of the Detailed Breakdown of the ABC be attached as supporting document in the payment of PPAs in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer:
to submit to the Municipal Accountant the above-mentioned disbursement vouchers and cancelled checks for the latter to take up the transactions in the books of the agency and subsequently submit it to the Audit Team for audit to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance; and
for internal control purposes, stop the practice of allowing claimants to have possession of paid Disbursement Vouchers to prevent misplacement of the same.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide for the disclosure of the road network of the LGU in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with COA Circular 2015-008.
We recommended that that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer to furnish the Audit Team with the RAAO and SAAOB as support to the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Point System to:
set up and maintain the GAD database with adequate, updated and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;
undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects, and activities are responsive to the said issues; and
revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.
We recommended that Management ensure that all proposed projects under the Local Development Fund be implemented within the budget year.

concerned LGU Officials: a.
submit to the COA, the
guidelines for the grant of
educational assistance to the
recipients and the complete
supporting documents for the
grants for 2014. Failure to
comply with the same may
result to audit suspensions; b.
conduct needs assessment for
education of every barangay to
ensure equitable distribution and
availability of the grant to
eligible recipients; c. establish a
more concrete and organized
scholarship program, taking into
consideration the needs
identified and the availability of
funds for the purpose; d. include
in the annual budget, a provision
for the educational assistance
grant; and e. strictly abide by the
government accounting rules
and regulations on the grant of
the same.
observations.managementAction:
No action taken. The
same practice had been
committed in 2016.

The control for the grant
rests upon the Municipal
Mayor, who, as needed
by circumstances,
accommodates requests
of this kind.
